[2018-04-13 20:51] kaelii: Loki probably deserves exhaustion and maybe also a longer build time.

[2018-04-13 20:52] crash_overlord: I'm confused what loki even does
[2018-04-13 20:52] kaelii: Targeting an opponent's unit, no point to targeting your own unit.
[2018-04-13 20:53] darkeisbein: basically freezes your opponents centurion and gives you 6 barriers and two Gauss charges
[2018-04-13 20:53] darkeisbein: a.k.a. removes centurion from the set
[2018-04-13 20:53] kaelii: Loki freezes an enemy blocker, and gives you 'fake' copy of that blocker.
[2018-04-13 20:53] tophwells (Toph): Targeting an opponent's unit is SDR breaking. Targeting a friendly unit would, I dunno, let you copy your Protoplasms to sacrifice them next turn.
[2018-04-13 20:54] darkeisbein: oh right breaks SDR even
[2018-04-13 20:54] tophwells (Toph): but OK I see the intention now
[2018-04-13 20:54] crash_overlord: Cant chill own units
[2018-04-13 20:55] darkeisbein: I mean I get it current engine limitations
[2018-04-13 20:55] kaelii: I really only put the HP limit because of Dgrid, which I think is the only blocker that spawns something each turn and thus would have lead to an unbounded state.
[2018-04-13 20:55] crash_overlord: Not really an engine limitation
[2018-04-13 20:55] crash_overlord: But the "chill" keyword is enemy blocker specific
[2018-04-13 20:55] crash_overlord: You could say "chill ANY blocker" but uh 🤔
[2018-04-13 20:56] tophwells (Toph): but yeah chill doesn't work that way
[2018-04-13 20:56] tophwells (Toph): would this also make the copied unit unclickable?
[2018-04-13 20:56] tophwells (Toph): because if you click it, it's not a blocker any more
[2018-04-13 20:56] apooche: If attack is free the unit is good at low inflation, even if energy isn’t free
[2018-04-13 20:57] crash_overlord: Yeah but like, how good :monkaS:
[2018-04-13 20:57] crash_overlord: Is it slightly better than tarsier good
[2018-04-13 20:58] crash_overlord: I'd guess it's probably much worse than it shows in most games though huh
[2018-04-13 20:58] crash_overlord: But it would make it quite degenerate in dgrid sets
[2018-04-13 20:58] darkeisbein: like buying an animus and 2 engies a turn isn't that big of an econ disadvantagew
[2018-04-13 20:58] darkeisbein: you should be able to defend with that
[2018-04-13 20:58] apooche: If energy is free as well, it is still good at R = 1.47 (roughly 10 gold for 2 attackers)
[2018-04-13 20:58] crash_overlord: Assuming the game lasts another 7 turns :clordSmart:
[2018-04-13 20:58] apooche: So, probably just OP
[2018-04-13 21:00] apooche: I mean, assume p2 A/TT/TD/11A I think game is going to turn 11
[2018-04-13 21:00] crash_overlord: Yeah probably just op
[2018-04-13 21:00] kaelii: Dat double animus line
[2018-04-13 21:01] apooche: It is decent at low inflation still
[2018-04-13 21:01] kaelii: I mean I guess you do get to jam 111TD or something.
[2018-04-13 21:01] apooche: Oh it should be 11AE
[2018-04-13 21:03] apooche: At low inflation, treating energy as half a gold, it is like 11 gold for 2 attack at bt8
[2018-04-13 21:03] crash_overlord: That sounds pretty ok
[2018-04-13 21:03] crash_overlord: I like the design :monkaS:

[2018-04-13 21:03] crash_overlord: I just don't want it being too strong
[2018-04-13 21:03] tophwells (Toph): wait, Crash_overlord priced that at Buildtime 7 without a calculator and it turned out more-or-less balanced?
[2018-04-13 21:04] crash_overlord: and bt7 is pretty op
[2018-04-13 21:04] tophwells (Toph): That's some hecka intuition for balance calulators!
[2018-04-13 21:04] crash_overlord: You kinda get there after thousands of games played :monkaS:
[2018-04-13 21:05] apooche: He also might have just calculated
[2018-04-13 21:05] crash_overlord: Buildtime makes everything way harder to price based on intuition though :NotLikeThis:
[2018-04-13 21:06] kaelii: Assuming we allow an SDR violation from Loki (creating the temporary copy of enemy's unit), is there a value of health he COULD :Chill: and possibly some amount of :Exhaust: after use where he would be balanced?
[2018-04-13 21:07] apooche: I didn’t do anything that fancy. Just multiplied by R^6 or R^7 to compare to normal bt1 attackers for various R in [1.33, 1.5]
[2018-04-13 21:07] darkeisbein: probably? But I still don't like it because it just removes big absorbers from the set due to the sdr violations
[2018-04-13 21:07] apooche: It isn’t sdr violating
[2018-04-13 21:07] apooche: Oh never mind me then
[2018-04-13 21:11] darkeisbein: like the biggest problem with Loki is, you can balance it around, let's say walls
[2018-04-13 21:11] darkeisbein: and then it is just 3 bonus absorb
[2018-04-13 21:12] darkeisbein: that also removes 4-6 health blockers from the set
[2018-04-13 21:15] kaelii: I can see it removing one supply 4-6 health blockers, and only sort of? If your opponent mirrors loki and he freezes the doomed version he now has an actual blocker AND the doomed blocker. If you freeze the doomed blocker you get full absorb. If you freeze the real one, he gets to use a smaller absorber.
[2018-04-13 21:16] darkeisbein: but then it just becomes a degnerate 10 absorb game if both players buy an E-matrix
[2018-04-13 21:16] darkeisbein: well not really I guess
[2018-04-13 21:16] darkeisbein: both just click the loki copies
[2018-04-13 21:16] darkeisbein: so essentially they just cancel each other out
[2018-04-13 21:17] kaelii: Defensively at least. Centurion would still contribute 2 attack.
[2018-04-13 21:17] darkeisbein: I didn't really see the mirror loki just cancelling eachother out before
[2018-04-13 21:18] darkeisbein: but that's also really weird...
[2018-04-13 21:18] kaelii: If you're both freezing wall 'illusions', do I want to build an Ematrix?
[2018-04-13 21:19] kaelii: Like, you can then freeze the Ematrix but I get value off the fake wall, while I freeze your ematrix fake.
[2018-04-13 21:19] darkeisbein: you get 3 extra absorb by your wall not getting frozen
[2018-04-13 21:19] darkeisbein: so I guess you get it
[2018-04-13 21:20] darkeisbein: or more likely your opponent still freezes your wall clone and you get 2 extra absorb
[2018-04-13 21:20] kaelii: Yeah, so it depends on how much bigger your absorber is? Going to Centurion means both are +3 absorb, but they get centurion damage for freezing it.

[2018-04-15 00:11] masn6811 (Masne98888): but it would also win if your opponent went breachproof, you killed all their drones, then snuck this out
[2018-04-15 00:12] masn6811 (Masne98888): or they kileld all their drones for you, eg. a venge all in
[2018-04-15 00:12] masn6811 (Masne98888): that's why if you want to make really big buildtime bombs, you should have them have buildtime of 1 less than they should, and have them sac themselves at the start of your turn to make gauss charges
[2018-04-15 00:12] masn6811 (Masne98888): so they can be killed better when being breached
[2018-04-15 00:13] masn6811 (Masne98888): alternatively, just make them have 1 hp during buildtime but do more than 1 damage
[2018-04-15 00:13] masn6811 (Masne98888): the longer the buildtime discrpancy, the more lopsided the ratio should be
[2018-04-15 00:13] masn6811 (Masne98888): but prismata isn't quite into that design territory yet 🤔

[2018-04-15 00:27] masn6811 (Masne98888): okay, i have an idea for a keyword: tramp- oh wait. okay, i have an idea for a keyword: non-trampling

[2018-04-15 00:28] masn6811 (Masne98888): while if you use it while breaching, you might be forced to overkill
[2018-04-15 00:28] masn6811 (Masne98888): can i have an example where it breaks SDR?
[2018-04-15 00:28] darkeisbein: oh w8 I thought non-trampling means can't breach 😄
[2018-04-15 00:28] f300xen (f0x): can't you just use sniping for basically the same effect
[2018-04-15 00:29] masn6811 (Masne98888): you breach if and only if your opponent has no way to assign the damage without losing defenses, in which case you assign damage at will
[2018-04-15 00:29] masn6811 (Masne98888): @f0x can't. because it's a drawback mechanic, and your opponent can take advantage of it
[2018-04-15 00:30] masn6811 (Masne98888): so to put non-trampling in perspective, splitter gives [1]. omega splitter gives [1][1][1]. a non-trampling omega splitter would give [3]
[2018-04-15 00:30] masn6811 (Masne98888): so if you attack with omega against a stack of walls, he chooses a wall to defend on, and [1]s get assigned to it until they run out or it dies
[2018-04-15 00:31] darkeisbein: seems to get countered by buying an engineer a turn :/
[2018-04-15 00:31] masn6811 (Masne98888): well, here's where ordering is important
[2018-04-15 00:31] masn6811 (Masne98888): say you have an non-trampling omega splitter and 2 normal splitters
[2018-04-15 00:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): your opponent has a wall and 2 engineers
[2018-04-15 00:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): if you order them [1][1][3], he can lose the wall for it
[2018-04-15 00:32] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Single sounds better.
[2018-04-15 00:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): while if you order them [3][1][1], he can sac an engi, then soak 2 on the wall
[2018-04-15 00:33] masn6811 (Masne98888): (of course [1][1][1] is strictly better than [3], that's the point)
[2018-04-15 00:33] masn6811 (Masne98888): while [3] is strictly better than [1]

[2018-04-15 00:35] f300xen (f0x): it's slightly different, but not enough to justify all these weird interactions
[2018-04-15 00:36] masn6811 (Masne98888): really complex, yeah
[2018-04-15 00:36] masn6811 (Masne98888): but trying to just show it could be done
[2018-04-15 00:37] masn6811 (Masne98888): i guess the most consistent breaching behavior is you get whatever chunks remain of your ordering to assign as you choose after your opponent soaks as many chunks as possible with defending
[2018-04-15 00:37] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): But how's used in the UI.
[2018-04-15 00:39] masn6811 (Masne98888): imagine gauss killer, a hypothetical unit that attacks for a single chunk of [5]
[2018-04-15 00:39] masn6811 (Masne98888): now if you aren't breaching, you can imagine it really just attacking for 1 into an engineer
[2018-04-15 00:39] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Spread the 1s, 2s, etc...
[2018-04-15 00:39] masn6811 (Masne98888): however, if you are clever, you can end your attack on it
[2018-04-15 00:39] masn6811 (Masne98888): which makes it really ahrd to absorb
[2018-04-15 00:40] masn6811 (Masne98888): what they might want to do is absorb 2 on the wall, then let the gauss killer kill it
[2018-04-15 00:40] masn6811 (Masne98888): however, if you end your attack on two chunks of [5], that's not an option
[2018-04-15 00:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): however, fi you do that, they can do the wall trick on the previous chunk of [5], then lose the last [5] to an engi, for the same result
[2018-04-15 00:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): if you want to stop yourself from getting cheesed by engis, what you shuld instead do is alternate, going [1] [5] [1] [5]...
[2018-04-15 00:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): that way, they have to choose between only absorbing 1 on the walls, or saccing two engis to a [1] and a [5]

[2018-04-15 00:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): finally, by ending on a [5], it makes it really hard for them to get defense grid absorb

[2018-04-15 00:43] masn6811 (Masne98888): and of course they can take 6 on the grid and soak it there, but they ose the grid

[2018-04-15 00:44] masn6811 (Masne98888): you only breach if they have no way to defend

[2018-04-15 05:35] kaelii: I feel like constructing Goose would be really annoying for breachproof: Converting 1 health drones into 5 health goose cannons seems like a really strong breachproof thing, especially considering this uses conduits and green which don't get 'wasted' the same way blue can be and are cheaper to build.
[2018-04-15 05:35] http404error: Is that intended as a submission?
[2018-04-15 05:35] kaelii: Not unless you really like it. 😛
[2018-04-15 05:39] kaelii: I think it's probably somewhere closer to clusterbolt in use -- you buy a couple conduits early on and later can swap over to resonators around the same time you have your goose cannons/etc stetup to slam charges out of your drones.
[2018-04-15 05:43] kaelii: There might be some pressure lines where you buy drone+click 2-3 per turn or something.
[2018-04-15 05:51] .bky_1556 (bky): "Converting 1 health drones into 5 health goose cannons seems like a really strong breachproof thing" -> Compare Steelforge; this is essentially a green Steelforge.
[2018-04-15 05:52] .bky_1556 (bky): Which probably makes it stronger than Steelforge as a click threat, because GG- is cheaper than BB-.
[2018-04-15 05:53] kaelii: It is: Green also doesn't get wasted at end of turn and goose cannons are stronger breachproof units than steel splitters. Downside is you can't use the cannons as makeshift walls to delay breaches like you might be able to with steelforge.
[2018-04-15 05:54] .bky_1556 (bky): Steelforge compensates by building absorbers and making BBB units more playable, ofc.
[2018-04-15 05:56] kaelii: Yeah, this 'just' makes extra conduits a bit cheaper and doesn't enable fairly easy access to 20-30 HP of attackers.
[2018-04-15 05:57] kaelii: It notably doesn't really help you spend down an excess of green just allows you to sink what it produces.
